name: countifi-master-2026
description: Countifi's reusable MASTER government proposal — real-customer test corpus for the PR-C5 proposal evaluator (govEvaluationService).

source.author: David H. Hailey — Founder & CEO, Countifi (david@countifi.com)
source.note: Deliberately generic master: blank Submitted-to / RFP# / date fields, 16 sections, meant to be tailored per solicitation. The original file is NOT in this repo and must never be edited.

cleaning.method: Two non-content paragraphs removed from word/document.xml in a copy of the original; repackaged as countifi-master-cleaned.docx. No other content altered.

removedArtifacts.location: Between 'API Integration' (end of Core Competencies) and Section 3
removedArtifacts.text: Perfect. From here onward, the proposal starts looking like a real government proposal. This section should be reused in nearly every solicitation with only minor customization.
removedArtifacts.why: Pasted AI-chatbot reply left in the body. Must never survive into any tailored output.

removedArtifacts.text: This matrix should be updated for each solicitation to map proposal content to the specific RFP requirements.
removedArtifacts.why: Internal template guidance, not proposal content.

textExtraction.tool: mammoth.extractRawText — the same code path as manualUploadService.extractTextFromDocx, so countifi-master-cleaned.txt is byte-identical to what the evaluator sees

evaluatorNotes.capObservation: This real proposal is ~50.7k chars; Pass B (DRAFT_TEXT_CAP=30000) truncates mid-Section-7, so 9 of 16 sections — including Security, Relevant Experience, and the Compliance Matrix — never reach the model.
evaluatorNotes.substringPitfall: Pass A matching is substring-based (draft.includes(keyword)). Bare 'nist' false-positives against 'administrator'. Probes below use specific phrases ('nist 800-53') for this reason.

expectedFindings.summary: Section 12 (Relevant Experience) is pure capability prose — zero named projects, clients, or references. Past performance is usually the heaviest-weighted eval factor.
Section 9 (Security & Data Protection) names no certification or compliance framework.
Section 15 self-declares 'Compliant' against nine generic headings, not actual RFP requirements.
Submitted-to, RFP #, and Submission date are blank; pricing exists only as an appendix placeholder ('Pricing Narrative').

passAProbes.text: Offeror must hold or be pursuing FedRAMP authorization
Describe alignment with NIST 800-53 or NIST 800-171 controls
Provide SOC 2 attestation and CJIS compliance where applicable
Provide three past performance references with client names
Contractor shall conduct user acceptance testing with a documented test plan
Describe risk management approach including a maintained risk register
Submit a cost proposal with a detailed price schedule
